seoTitle: How to Create a Report in Freshdesk
metaDescription: Learn how to build a custom report in Freshdesk to track ticket volume, agent performance, and support trends over time.
intro: Reports help you understand how your support team is performing and where bottlenecks show up. This guide walks through creating a custom report in Freshdesk.

steps.heading: Open the reporting section
steps.body: Navigate to the analytics or reports area of your Freshdesk dashboard.

steps.heading: Choose a report type
steps.body: Select from available report templates, such as ticket volume, resolution time, or agent performance.

steps.heading: Customize the metrics and filters
steps.body: Adjust the date range, ticket properties, and grouping options to focus the report on what matters to you.

steps.heading: Save or export the report
steps.body: Save the report for future reference or export it to share with your team or leadership.

faq.question: What kinds of reports can I create in Freshdesk?
faq.answer: You can build reports covering ticket volume, response times, resolution times, agent workload, and customer satisfaction.

faq.question: Can I schedule reports to run automatically?
faq.answer: Many Freshdesk plans support scheduled reports that are generated and sent automatically on a recurring basis.

faq.question: Can I export reports to share outside Freshdesk?
faq.answer: Yes, reports can typically be exported as files to share with people who don't have Freshdesk access.

faq.question: Are custom reports available on all plans?
faq.answer: Advanced custom reporting features are often tied to higher-tier plans, so check your plan's feature list.
